title: Shaft flex: the spine of your set
microFact: If your driver carries under ~200m, you're a Regular flex player. Buying Stiff 'to grow into' costs you distance today.
lesson:
The SHAFT bends during your swing and unloads at impact. FLEX ratings — Ladies (L), Senior (A), Regular (R), Stiff (S), Extra Stiff (X) — describe how much.

Too stiff for your speed: the shaft never unloads → low, weak shots right, harsh feel. Too soft: high spinny shots left, inconsistency. Matched: effortless height and your real distance.

Rough driver-swing-speed guide: under 75 mph → Senior; 75–95 mph (driver carry roughly 170–210m) → REGULAR; 95–105 → Stiff. Most 110-shooters are Regular, some Senior — and there is zero shame in the right shaft, only in the wrong one.

Also decide GRAPHITE vs STEEL for irons: graphite is lighter, easier to swing, easier on the joints; light steel gives more feedback and consistency. At your stage, either works — lightweight steel (or graphite if you value comfort) in Regular is the smart default. A launch monitor fitting settles it in 20 minutes; ask for exactly that at Clubworks.

quiz.question: Your shots with a too-stiff shaft will typically fly…
options: Low and to the right, feeling harsh
Exactly the same — flex is marketing
quiz.explanation: A shaft that's too stiff doesn't unload for you: low launch, right miss (for a right-hander), dead feel.
